creator: andrew@netplex.net
text:
YES, that patch fixes the kernel crash problem with >10 interfaces (tested on 14.0-RC3, and would think it applies to 13 also). THANKS!

But, it exposes a new problem. Having Xen allocate many more interfaces (>15 total) now produces an error: "xn15: failed to allocate tx grant refs"

That would be fine, but it causes a problem on reboot when recognizing the 16th interface: "run_interrupt_driven_hooks: still waiting after 60 seconds for xenbusb_nop_confighook_cb"

It seems the system partially allocates a new "xn15" interface (the 16th) and then the system has problems working with it. The kernel should just completely refuse to create/allocate the interface that is beyond the supported limit.
